PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-12-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Kane and Stanley Frazier for Gregory Price.

Mr. Kane stated that there was only one asset which was the Raymond James account, that an agreement had been reached with Everett Price's wife and son, and that all are onboard. Mr. Frazier stated that the real property was in the Trust and that Raymond James account was not in the Trust. The Court stated that the circumstances were unusual. Mr. Kane stated that Mr. Price had cancer and that he wanted to take care of his children. Mr. Frazier noted that the children were not provided for in the Trust. In addition, Mr. Frazier stated that they had the will in their office, that it had been scanned, but that they could not find the original will and other documents. Mr. Kane stated that Mr. Price asked for them to take the original will to their office. The Court noted that there was no appearance by Mr. Price's spouse or son. Mr. Kane stated that they all were aware of the circumstances regarding the execution of the will. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-24-2013 Continued To Aug-06-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
